title: 10 best herbal teas for one side headache
intro_desc: Herbal teas for one side headache are a natural remedy that can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with a unilateral headache. These teas work by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and improving circulation to help alleviate tension on one side of the head. Examples of herbal teas that have been shown to be effective in treating one-sided headaches include feverfew, ginger, and lavender. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing rapid relief from pain, allowing individuals to continue with their daily activities without interruption or discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew teas helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in feverfew, such as parthenolide, constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling around affected areas, thereby alleviating pain and pressure on the nerves. Additionally, feverfew's vasodilatory effects can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, which can also contribute to a decrease in headache intensity and frequency. By addressing the underlying causes of one-sided headaches, feverfew teas offer a natural and effective way to find relief from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew tea contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels, potentially alleviating headache symptoms.

Vasodilatory properties: The tea's flavonoids and terpenes can help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the affected area and potentially relieving constriction or spasm that may be contributing to the headache.

Antispasmodic effects: Feverfew's essential oil and other compounds have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, which may help ease tension headaches, including those characterized by one-sided pain, by reducing spasms in blood vessels and facial muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve tension and reduce pain associated with one-sided headaches.
Leaves: Containing parthenolide, a bioactive compound that has analgesic and anti-spasmodic effects, making it effective in reducing headache severity and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried tansy leaves measuring one tablespoon for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan to boiling point and then reduce the heat to simmer.
Add the measured tansy leaves to the hot water and allow it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, with the recommended dosage being one cup every two to three hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with one side headache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint relaxes tense muscles, reducing pressure on blood vessels and alleviating pain in specific areas. Additionally, the tea's cooling sensation can help to ease tension and spasms around the affected area, promoting relaxation and relief from discomfort. By incorporating herbal peppermint teas into one's routine, individuals may find natural respite from the discomfort of a one-sided headache.
remedy_properties: Cooled Analgesic Effect: Peppermint tea's menthol content helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area. This can be especially helpful for people who experience one-sided headaches due to tension or migraines.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by headaches. These compounds may also help relax tense muscles in the neck and scalp that contribute to one-sided headache pain.

Calming Antispasmodic Effects: The volatile oils present in peppermint tea, such as menthol and eucalyptol, have antispasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle tension and spasms in the head and neck, which are common contributors to one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, peppermint roots can help relieve tension and ease pain on one side of the head.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of menthone, peppermint rhyzomes can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headaches that affect one side of the head.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves into a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat 8 oz of water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a temperature of 200°f.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea mixture through the infuser or strainer into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before drinking it slowly and enjoying its soothing effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow teas helps with one side headache because it contains salicin, a natural pain-relieving compound similar to aspirin. When consumed as a tea, white willow's active ingredients are absorbed into the bloodstream and travel directly to the affected area, providing targeted relief from tension and pain. The ant-inflammatory properties of white willow help to reduce swelling and ease muscle contractions, allowing for quick and effective alleviation of one-sided headaches caused by stress, tension, or sinus issues.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tea contains salicin, a natural pain-reliever that works similarly to aspirin, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve headache symptoms.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in white willow tea may help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to headaches and migraines.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White willow tea contains a variety of anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels and nerves that may be contributing to one-sided headache pain.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the head, making it effective for treating one side headaches.
Roots: The roots of the white willow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the nerves, providing relief from one side headaches.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried white willow bark (salix alba) 2 tablespoons, boiling water and honey to taste.
Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried white willow bark into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the white willow bark allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well before drinking immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease tension in the blood vessels. The tea's natural analgesic properties also work to block pain pathways in the brain, providing quick relief from the throbbing sensation. Additionally, ginger's warming effects help to stimulate blood flow, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach affected areas, further reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain and swelling associated with headaches, providing relief from discomfort.
Digestive aid: Ginger has natural digestive benefits that can help alleviate nausea and stomach upset, common symptoms often accompanying one-sided head pain.
Pain relief agents: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce headache severity and frequency.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, making them an effective remedy for one-sided headaches.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain compounds that can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which may help alleviate tension and pressure that can lead to one-sided headaches.
remedy_recipe: Measure 200 milliliters of boiling water into a large cup for steeping the tea.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root powder or fresh grated ginger to the hot water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes in order to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately, taking small sips every 30 minutes as needed for headache relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with one side headache because of its natural calming properties, which can effectively soothe a throbbing temple. The tea's mild sedative qualities can help relax tense muscles and ease blood flow to the affected area, reducing tension and pressure that may be contributing to the headache. Additionally, lavender's subtle aroma can promote a sense of tranquility, further alleviating stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to headache relief by calming irritated nerves and blood vessels.

Calming effects: The relaxing and sedative properties of lavender tea can help ease tension and anxiety, common triggers for one-sided headaches, by promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress levels.
Pain-relieving compounds: Lavender tea contains a small amount of camphor, a natural pain reliever that may help reduce headache pain and discomfort in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm tension in the affected side of the head, reducing pain.
Flowers: Dried lavender flowers contain a calming essential oil that helps to relax the mind and body, reducing migraine-like symptoms on one side of the head.
remedy_recipe: Boil fresh water in a kettle or pot for 3-4 minutes before preparing the tea.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the lavandula angustifolia flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink the tea immediately while it is still warm, taking small sips every 15-20 minutes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with one side headache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and cineole, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the affected area. This targeted relief can help alleviate pressure points that often contribute to one-sided headaches. Additionally, the calming effects of the tea's menthol content can soothe and relax the mind and body, further reducing headache severity. As a natural and non-pharmaceutical solution, Tasmanian blue gum teas provides a holistic approach to managing one-side headaches.
remedy_properties: Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid is a potent anti-inflammatory compound found in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea that helps reduce pain and inflammation in the body, making it effective in relieving one-sided headaches.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that can help relieve tension and pressure in the head and neck, providing relief from one-sided headache symptoms.

Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which means it can help relax and calm down muscle contractions in the head and neck that may be contributing to one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with one-sided headaches.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant have been traditionally used to treat headaches due to their analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties, which help to ease tension and relieve headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves or purchase dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in bulk for a single serving.
Boil one quart of filtered water and steep one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves or three sprigs of fresh leaves for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-resistant cup to remove loose leaves and particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking, taking about 5-7 minutes at room temperature.
Drink the eucalyptus tea within the hour of preparation while still warm for maximum relief from one side headache symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with one side headache because its soothing properties can effectively calm and relax the affected area. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the blood vessels, which may be contributing to the headache. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of one-sided headaches. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile teas offer a natural and effective solution for relief from one side headache pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and relieve pain associated with one-sided headaches.

Muscle relaxants: Chamomile has been shown to possess muscle-relaxing properties, which can help alleviate tension headaches by relaxing tense muscles that may be contributing to the headache's location on one side of the head.

Anxiolytic effects: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of one-sided headaches, and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat tension and stress-related headaches, including onesided pain.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate one-sided headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Boil fresh water in a saucepan and let it cool for five minutes.
Pour the cooled water over the measured herbs in a teapot.
Steep the tea mixture for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with one side headache because of its natural ability to soothe and relax the nervous system. The active ingredients in valerian root, such as valeric acid and isovaleric acid, work together to calm the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that can often lead to one-sided headaches. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle contractions, herbal valerian teas can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with one side headaches, providing relief from what can be a debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant: Valerian tea's muscle-relaxing properties can help ease tension headaches by relaxing the blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which may be contributing to the headache.

Anxiolytic (Anxiety-Relieving): Valerian tea's anxiolytic effects can help calm anxiety and stress, which are common triggers of one-sided headaches, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of the headache.
Antispasmodic: Valerian tea's antispasmodic properties can help relieve spasms in blood vessels and nerves that may be causing or exacerbating the one-sided headache.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving one-side headache pain.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, both of which can alleviate one-side headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root per cup of boiling water.
Steep the valeriana mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow its active compounds release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste if desired and stir well.
Drink the warm tea slowly and savor it for at least 20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops teas helps with one side headache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in hops, such as xanthohumol and humulone, work together to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. Additionally, hops have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to ease tension and promote relaxation, further alleviating headache symptoms. Drinking hops tea can provide rapid relief from one-sided headaches, promoting a sense of well-being and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Hops tea helps to relax muscle tension, which can contribute to one-sided headaches by reducing spasms in the neck and shoulder muscles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in hops tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and tissues of the head and neck, potentially alleviating headache pain.

Sedative properties: Hops tea contains sedative compounds like humulone and lupulone, which can help calm the nervous system, relax tense muscles, and promote a sense of calmness, reducing the frequency and severity of one-sided headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hops roots are known for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving tension and pain associated with one-sided headaches.
Leaves: The leaves of the hops plant contain flavonoids and volatile oils that can help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them a natural remedy for soothing one-sided head pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried hops flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of loose-leaf hops tea blend.
Measure 250ml boiling water into a teapot and steep the hops for 8-10 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add one teaspoon of freshly grated ginger root to enhance calming properties and stir well.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with one side headache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work together to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and relax tense muscles, which can help alleviate tension headaches that often cause pain on one side of the head. By soothing and calming the blood vessels and surrounding tissues, yarrow teas can effectively ease the pain and discomfort associated with one-sided headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as acetyleugenol and chamazulene, help reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which may contribute to one-sided headache relief.

Circulatory stimulant: The bioactive compounds in yarrow tea, including apiol and origanelin, stimulate blood circulation and dilate blood vessels, which can help alleviate tension and constricted vessels that may be contributing to the headache.

Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea's antispasmodic properties, particularly due to its flavonoids and terpenoids, relax muscles and relieve spasms in the face, neck, and head, helping to ease one-sided tension headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and lignans, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain.
Flowers: Contain sesquiterpene lactones that can relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of achillea millefolium and measure out 2 teaspoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat water in a pot or kettle to boiling point then remove from heat source within 5 minutes.
Steep the herb leaves in the hot water for 10-15 minutes to allow flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink immediately while still warm or within 30 minutes of preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat one side headache with herbal teas is by using feverfew tea, which contains parthenolide that helps relieve tension and reduces inflammation. Peppermint tea is another effective option as it has a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to ease stress and promote relaxation. Additionally, ginger tea can be used to alleviate pain and reduce nausea associated with headaches.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to one-sided headaches that are treated with herbal teas are migraines, sinusitis, and neuralgia. Herbs such as feverfew, willow bark, and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve pain and reduce swelling in the affected area. Other herbs like passionflower and skullcap can calm nervous tension and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate symptoms of one-sided headaches.
